module.name: scanner
module.spec: FileScanner follows imports starting from main.pin.xml, parses each .pin.xml as XML, extracts spec elements with their id attribute, SHA1-hashes the trimmed inner content, and returns Spec records with module-qualified ID, Hash, Filepath, and LineNumber.

FileScanner walks the project directory for code files by extension, finds lines matching the D! id=shortcode range-start and D! id=shortcode range-end pattern, pairs them by ID within the same file, SHA1-hashes the lines between range-start and range-end (exclusive of both marker lines) with other marker declarations blanked, and returns Marker records with ID, Hash, Filepath, LineNumber, and EndLineNumber.
All hashing uses SHA1 hex-encoded via crypto/sha1 and fmt percent-x formatting. The hash is deterministic across runs for identical content.
Duplicate spec IDs within the same module cause an error with the duplicate ID quoted.
Duplicate marker shortcodes across files cause an error with the duplicate shortcode quoted.
A spec element without an id attribute causes an error.

Only files with recognized code extensions are scanned for markers: .go, .py, .js, .ts, .jsx, .tsx, .java, .c, .cpp, .h, .hpp, .rs, .rb, .php, .swift, .kt, .cs, .scala, .sh, .bash, .lua, .dart, .vue, .svelte.

If spec elements are nested inside a specs wrapper element under main or module, the scanner returns an error explaining that specs must be direct children of the root element, not inside a specs wrapper.

The id attribute in a spec element must not contain a dot. The scanner constructs the fully-qualified spec ID as moduleName + "." + localId. This ensures every qualified spec ID has exactly one dot, separating module from local ID.

Marker shortcodes must not contain a dot. The scanner rejects any marker ID containing a dot with an error. Dots are reserved for spec ID qualification, enabling unambiguous disambiguation in CLI commands.

Every marker must declare either range-start or range-end as a suffix. Old-style markers without a suffix are rejected with an error. Each range-start with ID X must have a matching range-end with ID X in the same file appearing after the start. Each range-end with ID X must have a matching range-start with ID X in the same file appearing before the end. The scanner reports all unpaired markers at once, not fail-on-first.

Before hashing a marker's range, every line within the range that matches the marker pattern has its D! declaration stripped (from D! to end of line), leaving only the comment prefix. This makes markers invisible to each other's hashes, enabling nested and overlapping ranges without interference.
